question.text: In the context of retirement planning, what does the term 'time horizon' encompass?

answer.text: Only the years remaining until retirement
Both the years to retirement (accumulation) AND the years during retirement (distribution)
The time since the client started working

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Time horizon in retirement planning has two components: (1) years to retirement — the accumulation period, and (2) years during retirement — the distribution period. Both must be planned for. **The Trap:** Focusing only on the years to retirement ignores the distribution phase, which could span 20-30 years. Both phases require distinct planning. **Cross-Exam Context:** The dual time horizon concept distinguishes retirement planning from other financial goals which typically have only one time horizon. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always discuss both horizons with clients. Many focus only on reaching retirement but have no plan for the potentially 25-30 years they will spend in retirement.
